what: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: opinionated ggplot2 extension executive-grade consulting output. Ships archetype themes, palettes, mechanical geom wrappers, (later releases) locale-aware label helpers data-aware polish layer. Heads : ggconsulting early development. public API shaped real consulting decks; expect breaking changes 0.x.

title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: development version GitHub:

title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: theme_strategy() routes palette’s main colour ggplot2 4.x’s from_theme() mechanism, unmapped geoms inherit automatically — scale_color_*() calls needed single-series case. ggconsulting also sets small set aesthetic defaults library() attach (currently geom_point size = 2.5). Opt via:
code: library(ggplot2) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2 library(ggconsulting) #> v ggconsulting set ggplot2 aesthetic defaults #> i Opt out: `ct_unset_defaults()` or `options(ggconsulting.autoload = FALSE)` #> i Column width / linewidth: use `ct_col()` / `ct_line()`, or apply #>   `ct_theme()`/`theme_strategy()` for linewidth via `from_theme()`  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(     title    = "Fuel efficiency vs. vehicle weight",     subtitle = "1974 Motor Trend data",     caption  = "Source: datasets::mtcars"   ) +   theme_strategy() options(ggconsulting.autoload = FALSE) # or, mid-session: ct_unset_defaults()

title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: ct_theme() / theme_strategy() — composable theme builder archetype preset. Built ggplot2 4.x theme_sub_*() helpers routed element_geom() from_theme() linkage. Five starter palettes — strategy_navy, strategy_emerald, strategy_crimson, strategy_azure, strategy_slate. ct_col() / ct_line() / ct_point() — mechanical wrappers consulting-grade defaults call-site overrides. ct_set_defaults() / ct_unset_defaults() — update_geom_defaults()-driven aesthetic baseline honest revert.

title: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for ggplot2
text: Next prompts add theme_finance() / theme_editorial() archetypes, scale_color_ct() / scale_fill_ct(), ct_finish() polish layer, locale-aware label helpers (fmt_brl, ct_locale), install_consulting_fonts(), vignettes, polished gallery.

what: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
title: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
text: ct_set_defaults() overrides ggplot2 aesthetic defaults via ggplot2::update_geom_defaults(). v0.1 sets single override: geom_point size = 2.5. ct_unset_defaults() restores whatever values active first time ct_set_defaults() ran — ggplot2's untouched baseline. Originals captured package-private environment, repeated ct_set_defaults() calls idempotent. Column width linewidth handled elsewhere — see ct_col() / ct_line() explicit formal overrides, apply ct_theme() / theme_strategy() linewidth via from_theme().

title: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults
text: functions return invisible(NULL).

title: Set or restore ggconsulting aesthetic defaults — ct_defaults

what: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
text: Three-line pass-throughs ship consulting-grade defaults ggplot2::geom_col() width, ggplot2::geom_line() linewidth, ggplot2::geom_point() size. Use want defaults baked call site; plain geom_line() also pick linewidth ct_theme() / theme_strategy() via from_theme(), geom_point() size autoloaded ct_set_defaults().

title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
code: ct_col(..., width = 0.8)  ct_line(..., linewidth = 0.7)  ct_point(..., size = 2.5)

title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
text: ... Forwarded underlying ggplot2 constructor. width Column width. Defaults 0.8 (vs ggplot2's 0.9). linewidth Line width. Defaults 0.7 (vs ggplot2's 0.5). size Point size. Defaults 2.5 (vs ggplot2's 1.5).

title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
text: ggplot2 ggplot2::layer() object.

title: Drop-in replacements for geom_col(), geom_line(), and geom_point() — ct_geoms
code: library(ggplot2) d <- data.frame(g = c("A", "B", "C"), v = c(3, 5, 2)) p_col   <- ggplot(d, aes(g, v)) + ct_col() p_line  <- ggplot(economics, aes(date, unemploy)) + ct_line() p_point <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + ct_point()

what: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
text: Flexible builder behind archetype presets (theme_strategy() friends). Composes ggplot2::theme_minimal() executive-output overrides via theme_sub_*() helpers, sets theme geom element from_theme()-aware layers (e.g. ggplot2::geom_line()) inherit resolved linewidth main colour, applies resolved font fallback chain.

title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
code: ct_theme(   palette = "strategy_navy",   font = "Inter",   density = c("normal", "tight", "loose"),   context = c("presentation", "report", "screen"),   base_size = NULL,   main_color = NULL )

title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
text: palette Palette name (e.g. "strategy_navy") character vector colours. font Preferred font family. Falls back c("Helvetica Neue", "Arial", "sans") unavailable. density One "normal", "tight", "loose". Controls panel.spacing axis-text margins. context One "presentation", "report", "screen". Drives default base_size plot.margin. base_size Base font size. NULL, derived context. main_color Colour used plot title routed theme geom element's ink slot, unmapped geoms pick via from_theme(). NULL falls back palette's first colour.

title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Build a consulting-grade ggplot2 theme — ct_theme
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   ct_theme()

title: ggconsulting: Executive-Grade Themes and Helpers for 'ggplot2' — ggconsulting-package
text: Maintainer: Vinicius Reginatto viniciusoike@gmail.com

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
text: Preset path ct_theme() tuned minimal, generous-whitespace, navy-default look inspired top-tier global strategy consultancies.

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
text: main_color Colour used plot title. Defaults strategy_navy palette's primary title matches downstream data marks. Pass value grDevices::col2rgb() accepts override. ... Forwarded ct_theme() — e.g. density, context, base_size.

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Strategy archetype theme — theme_strategy
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   theme_strategy()

title: ggconsulting (development version)
text: ct_theme() builder composed via theme_sub_*() helpers routed element_geom() from_theme()-aware geoms inherit palette’s main colour (ink) default linewidth. theme_strategy() archetype preset path. Five starter palettes: strategy_navy, strategy_emerald, strategy_crimson, strategy_azure, strategy_slate. ct_col() / ct_line() / ct_point() mechanical wrappers explicit-override use call site. ct_set_defaults() / ct_unset_defaults() true aesthetic defaults (currently geom_point size = 2.5), autoloaded library attach opt-option. has_font() internal helper.
